differences.title: 1. INITIAL RENDER
differences.leftDesc: Initial HTML sent from server, faster perceived load.
differences.rightDesc: Requires JavaScript to download and render UI.

differences.title: 2. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Can directly access server resources without API layer.
differences.rightDesc: Must use APIs or server functions to fetch data.

differences.title: 3. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Reduces JavaScript sent to the client, improving performance.
differences.rightDesc: Includes all necessary UI logic and state.

differences.title: 4. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Primarily for static content and initial rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Ideal for dynamic UIs, state management, and events.

differences.title: 5. SECURITY
differences.leftDesc: Sensitive logic and keys remain on the server.
differences.rightDesc: Logic is exposed in the browser's developer tools.

differences.title: 6. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State managed on the server, doesn't persist client-side.
differences.rightDesc: Requires robust client-side state management solutions.

differences.title: 7. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Content is readily available for search engine crawlers.
differences.rightDesc: Crawlers may struggle without JavaScript execution.
